person.name: Anita Ihuman
person.bio: Anita Ihuman is a developer advocate and community strategist with over 6+ years of experience driving contributions and growth of cloud-native tech and developer communities. Anita is a cloud evangelist promoting the growth and adoption of platform and cloud-native solutions across Africa, serving as an organizer for Kubernetes Community Days (KCD) Nigeria and the CNCF Abuja community group. She serves as a maintainer and Governing Board member at CHAOSS. She also contributes as an advisory board member at OpenUK, a member of the NumFOCUS DISC Committee, and proudly contributes to the DevNetwork Cloud Advisory Board—sharing insights that help shape the future of cloud technology.

person.name: Patrick Di Fazio
person.bio: I’m a security engineer specialized in red teaming and offensive security. I mainly focus on vulnerability assessments, penetration testing, security research, and Kubernetes container security.  With a strong Linux background and a deep belief in open source, I enjoy exploring architectural security and analyzing flaws in modern processors and embedded systems. I’m passionate about exploring and experimenting with security technologies, from low-level internals to high-level systems.  But most of all, I love flipping bits in your computer’s RAM.

person.name: Remco Stolwijk
person.bio: I'm a cloud-native engineer from the Netherlands. Since the start of my career, I've been working with Kubernetes and a wide range of CNCF projects. I love building scalable, modern platforms and exploring new open-source tech. You might spot me at KubeCon.

person.name: Chathuranga Wickramasinghe
person.bio: Hi there! I’m Chathuranga, a Cloud Architect passionate about building secure, scalable, and cloud-native platforms using Kubernetes and other CNCF technologies. I enjoy designing resilient multi-region architectures and enabling teams through automation, observability, and GitOps practices. My interests span Kubernetes operations, DevSecOps, and platform engineering, with a strong focus on creating reliable systems that empower developers to deliver confidently. I love sharing ideas, learning from others, and contributing to the broader cloud-native community.

person.name: Edith Puclla
person.bio: Edith is a Tech Evangelist at Percona Corporation, an open source contributor, and Docker Captain. She is a Docker and Kubernetes enthusiast who loves to create visual content. She became certified in KCNA and created a guide for others to become certified, too https://github.com/edithturn/KCNA-training. She also was a speaker at Docker Community All hands # 7, where she shared her experience in creating a Docker extension for Hoppscotch https://www.youtube.com / watch?V=uGADVJTodYE&t=14sWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because I like exploring the CNCF ecosystems and having fun doing it. It's also rewarding to share content with the community and meet people from all over the world. Thanks to the community, I have obtained many opportunities in my professional career, and now it is time to give back.

person.name: Gyeongcheol Shin
person.bio: With a 15-year background in cybersecurity, I operate as a freelance ISMS-P auditor, verifying compliance with Korea’s information security standards. My technical auditing covers a wide range of systems, including containerized architectures and CNCF-based infrastructures. I provide objective assessments to ensure that these evolving technologies maintain the necessary security integrity and governance

person.name: Varun Selvaraj
person.bio: Cloud-native and Kubernetes specialist focused on building scalable platforms for AI/ML and modern applications. Experienced in deploying GPU-powered workloads, distributed systems, and secure air-gapped environments using CNCF technologies. Strong interest in platform engineering, automation, and real-world enterprise use cases. Actively contribute through technical sessions and hands-on knowledge sharing. Passionate about bridging Kubernetes with next-generation AI infrastructure.

person.name: Hallef Alves
person.bio: Cloud Analyst at B3 Digitas, working with crypto environments inside Kubernetes, don't currently create any content but plans to.

person.name: Marco Maurer
person.bio: Grüezi from Switzerland!  After drawing schematics and layout PCBs, repairing power supplies and xDSL modems and writing assembler and C for microcontrollers as an electronics technician in 2005, I luckily came to the embedded Linux world: Linux based LAN switches, routers, PBXes.In 2015 I then switched into the public sector to the market leader in public bus transport here in Switzerland for modernizing the IT systems inside the ~2000 busses. We proved that microservices and containers aren't just limited to datacenters and the cloud but are also working great in the edge. Unfortunately there was no K8s for the edge like k3s at this time, so we used docker-compose.  Since 2019 I work as a platform engineer around Kubernetes and its ecosystem.
